title: 10 best herbal capsules for upper abdominal pain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for upper abdominal pain are natural supplements that alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the upper abdomen. They help by soothing the digestive system and reducing irritation. The best herbal capsules for this issue include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinnamomum verum, Ginkgo biloba, Cassia angustifolia, Angelica sinensis, Foeniculum vulgare, Piper nigrum, and Panax ginseng. If left untreated, upper abdominal pain can severely impact daily life, causing fatigue, anxiety, and digestive issues that hinder social and professional activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which has potent analgesic and antioxidant properties. Curcumin works by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and other conditions that cause stomach cramps, bloating, and discomfort. By soothing the digestive system, turmeric capsules provide natural relief from upper abdominal pain, allowing for improved digestion and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Action: Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation in the stomach and small intestine, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by conditions like gastritis, ulcers, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Antioxidant Properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

COX-2 Inhibitory Activity: Curcumin in turmeric capsules has been shown to inhibit COX-2 enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that cause pain and inflammation in the stomach and intestines, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the digestive system through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the severity of digestive inflammation.
Volatile Oil (Caryophyllene): It helps with upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the digestive system through its anti-inflammatory and gastroprotective properties.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are commonly used due to their high content of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots are also widely used as they contain a high concentration of curcumin and other beneficial compounds that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Seeds: Curcuma longa seeds are sometimes used as they contain a compound called demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure 300g of dried curcuma longa root powder and sift it through a fine mesh strainer.
Weigh out 150g of gelatin capsules and prepare them for filling using a capsule filling machine.
Mix the curcuma longa powder with 100ml of distilled water and 10ml of glycerin to create a uniform paste.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa paste using the capsule filling machine and a filling gauge.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for a minimum of 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The gingerols and shogaols present in these capsules work together to relax the muscles in the stomach and small intestine, reducing spasms and cramps associated with upper abdominal pain. Additionally, the capsules' natural warming effect increases blood flow and reduces inflammation in the area, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as cramps and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Ginger's antispasmodic properties help relax the smooth muscle lining the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause upper abdominal pain.

Digestive enzymes regulation: Ginger helps regulate digestive enzymes, which can become imbalanced and lead to upper abdominal pain due to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). By regulating these enzymes, ginger capsules may help ease symptoms of IBS-related pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from pain.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties, as well as analgesic and antispasmodic effects, which help to alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdominal region, while also relaxing the muscles to provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerol and shogaol, which provide anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.

Roots: Roots are another widely used part of Zingiber officinale, often used in combination with rhyzomes, to help alleviate upper abdominal pain and nausea due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used in herbal remedies for upper abdominal pain, although less commonly than rhyzomes or roots, due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of zingiber officinale when they are mature and the leaves begin to yellow.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Slice the cleaned roots into thin pieces to increase their surface area for drying.
Dry the sliced root pieces in a low-temperature oven or using a dehydrator for 24 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the dried root powder and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping. The menthol in peppermint also has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, which can help to calm inflammation and irritation that may be contributing to the discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and pain in the upper abdomen, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effect: Peppermint capsules relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain. This is because peppermint oil contains menthol, which has a calming effect on smooth muscle.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in peppermint capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as upper abdominal pain.

Gastroprotective effects: Peppermint capsules have been shown to have a protective effect on the mucous membranes lining the stomach and small intestine, reducing inflammation and damage caused by acid reflux and other conditions that can lead to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to numb the pain receptors in the abdominal muscles and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to the pain.

Menthone: Menthone is a terpene that has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that may be causing the upper abdominal pain.

Royleanone: Royleanone is a labdane diterpene that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and alleviate pain.

Roots: The roots are utilized for their rich content of menthol, menthone, and limonene, which have carminative and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce discomfort and inflammation in the upper abdomen.

Stems: The stems of the plant are also used due to their ability to retain the essential oils and bioactive compounds found in the leaves, making them a suitable alternative for creating capsules with similar pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at peak oil production in the morning.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 24 hours or less.
Grind 10 grams of dried mentha x piperita into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with 250-500 milligrams of ground mentha x piperita powder each.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach area. Additionally, chamomile has a gentle sedative effect on the muscles, relaxing any cramping or spasms that may be contributing to the pain. By promoting relaxation and calming the digestive system, chamomile capsules provide effective relief from upper abdominal pain caused by digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile capsules contain apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as cramps and discomfort.

Soothing and calming effect: The apigenin in chamomile also has a soothing and calming effect on the muscles and digestive tract, which can help to relax and calm the stomach, reducing feelings of discomfort and pain.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping in the stomach, which is common cause of upper abdominal pain, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive tract, which helps alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol, bisabolol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help calm the digestive system and reduce pain in the upper abdomen.

Matricine: A sesquiterpene lactone, matricine has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and alleviating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high apigenin and luteolin content, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Leaves: The leaves are utilized for their flavonoid content, particularly apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects that can help manage upper abdominal discomfort.

Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and calming effects that can help alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when they are dry and free of moisture.
Dry the matricaria chamomilla flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the fine powder with 500 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain compounds that stimulate digestion and improve liver function. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upper abdominal discomfort. Additionally, dandelion root has been shown to increase bile production, which can aid in reducing bloating and cramping associated with digestive issues. By supporting healthy digestion and liver function, dandelion capsules may provide relief from recurring upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to upper abdominal pain.
Choleretic effects: Dandelion stimulates bile production and release from the liver, which can help alleviate constipation, bloating, and cramping associated with upper abdominal pain.

Digestive stimulants: The bitter compounds in dandelion, such as taraxacoside, can stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion, reducing symptoms of upper abdominal pain, such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that contribute to pain relief and reduce inflammation in the upper abdominal region.
Taraxol: Taraxol, a sesquiterpene lactone, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the digestive system, alleviating upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of taraxasterol, a triterpene with anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Roots: Roots are used for their bitter compounds, including sesquiterpenes and sesquiterpene lactones, which may help stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve upper abdominal discomfort.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high content of flavonoids and coumarins, which may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help soothe upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present to preserve potency.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of the taraxacum officinale powder with 1/2 teaspoon of capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill 6-8 empty vegetable capsules with the taraxacum officinale powder mixture and seal them tightly to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because it contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps to heal and protect the liver cells. The liver is responsible for filtering toxins from the body, and when it becomes inflamed or damaged, it can cause upper abdominal pain. Milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the liver, alleviating discomfort and promoting overall digestive health. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from chronic liver disease-related abdominal pain without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid complex found in milk thistle seeds, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to abdominal pain relief.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and pain associated with upper abdominal issues.
Silycristin: Silycristin, another key component of milk thistle, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin helps with upper abdominal pain by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, reducing liver damage and inflammation that can cause pain in the upper abdomen.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in milk thistle help with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and improving liver function, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdomen.

Silibinin: Silibinin helps with upper abdominal pain by protecting the liver from damage and inflammation, improving liver function, and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that can cause pain in the upper abdomen.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain silymarin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and protect the liver, which can alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the upper abdomen.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules that contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of silybum marianum flowers and leaves from the plant when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered plant material with 1/2 teaspoon of gelatin and 1/4 teaspoon of water to create a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them using a capsule filling machine or a dropper.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and cramps in the digestive tract. The herb's calming effect on the nervous system also assists in soothing discomfort and tension in the upper abdomen, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and stomach cramps. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the digestive muscles, reducing contractions and spasms that can contribute to abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic activity: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms in the stomach, which can provide relief from upper abdominal pain.

Soothing effect on digestive tract: Lemon balm has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, which can help to calm inflammation and irritation in the stomach, intestines, and other parts of the GI tract that may be contributing to upper abdominal pain.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to many types of upper abdominal pain, including irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and protecting the digestive system from oxidative stress.
Nepetalactone: A terpene that has anti-anxiety and anti-spasmodic effects, helping to calm the digestive system and reduce muscle spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain.

Volatile oils (mainly geranial and linalool): Terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, helping to ease digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate upper abdominal pain by promoting the release of trapped gas and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system.

Flowers: They are used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve gas and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the melissa officinalis leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and the plant is healthy.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for about 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of cellulose powder and 10 milligrams of silicon dioxide.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal the capsules properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of their ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The root extract in licorice capsules contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and small intestine, which are common causes of upper abdominal pain. Additionally, licorice has natural antacid properties that help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Herbal licorice capsules contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the upper abdomen.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, a type of plant compound that acts as a natural antacid and soothes the mucous membranes in the stomach, helping to neutralize acidity and ease upper abdominal pain.

Saponins: Saponins are another key compound found in licorice root that helps to reduce inflammation and improve digestion by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that contribute to pain and inflammation in the upper abdomen.
Liquiritigenin: It helps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can alleviate pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.

18β-Glycyrrhetinic acid: It helps by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and reducing inflammation in the stomach and small intestine, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by acid reflux and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, is used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Barks: Licorice extract from the barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra is used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to its ability to reduce inflammation and provide antioxidant effects.
Seeds: Licorice saponins extracted from the seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own in a controlled environment.
Clean and dry the harvested roots using a food dehydrator at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered root with 500 milligrams of vegetarian capsule filler and 100 milligrams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100-count capsule molds with the mixture and seal each capsule using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its soothing properties that calm inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. The mucilage present in marshmallow root creates a protective barrier on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with acid reflux, gastritis, and ulcers. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the abdominal area, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes a healthy gut environment, alleviating upper abdominal pain and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains mucilage, a thick, protective coating that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the digestive tract by blocking pro-inflammatory enzymes and pathways.

Emollient properties: The mucilage in marshmallow root has emollient properties, which means it helps to soften and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain caused by conditions like gastritis or ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
Glycosides: Glycosides in Althaea officinalis have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the upper abdominal region.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to protect the digestive tract from damage and reduce the severity of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties for the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are utilized for their anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which help alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
Barks: Althaea officinalis barks are used for their mucilaginous properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried root of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of the ground root powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 5 grams of water to create a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the paste and seal them immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural ability to ease digestive discomfort. The active compound anethole in fennel has a calming effect on the smooth muscle lining in the stomach, which relaxes and soothes cramps, spasms, and bloating. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce inflammation, providing relief from upper abdominal pain caused by indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). By promoting a healthy digestive system, fennel capsules can effectively alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with upper abdominal issues.

Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to bloating, cramps, and pain in the upper abdomen, making it a natural remedy for digestive discomfort.
Spasmolytic effects: Fennel's spasmolytic properties help relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can cause abdominal pain, cramping, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone is a terpene found in Foeniculum vulgare that helps alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach and small intestine.

Anethole: Anethole is a phenolic compound present in Foeniculum vulgare that helps relieve upper abdominal pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which soothe the stomach muscles and reduce pain.

Beta-pinene: Beta-pinene is a terpene in Foeniculum vulgare that helps with upper abdominal pain by relaxing the stomach muscles, reducing inflammation, and preventing spasms, ultimately providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Leaves: They are used because of their antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and cramps in the upper abdomen, providing relief from pain.
Roots: They are used due to their bitter and antispasmodic properties, which help to stimulate digestion and reduce muscle spasms in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves from a clean environment ensuring proper identification.
Grind the harvested foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 5g of vegetable cellulose in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the foeniculum vulgare mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal foeniculum vulgare capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain eugenol, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When consumed as a capsule, eugenol is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected area of the stomach and small intestine, where it reduces inflammation and discomfort. This can provide relief from upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as indigestion, gastritis, or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_properties: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the upper abdomen.
Eugenol: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, eugenol helps to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion and nausea that often accompany upper abdominal pain.

Phenolic compounds: The phenolic compounds present in clove capsules have antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce bacterial growth and ease muscle spasms that contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the cloves plant contain a high concentration of eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Roots: The roots of the cloves plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, making them a popular choice for medicinal capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves measuring exactly 30 grams for this recipe.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 150°f for eight hours or overnight.
Grind the dried syzygium aromaticum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for ten minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground powder with two hundred milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for twenty-five minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with exactly five grams each of the prepared herbal mixture and seal tightly within three hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a capsule, this compound is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the stomach and intestines, where it reduces inflammation and relaxes the muscles that cause cramping and discomfort in the upper abdomen. Additionally, ceylon cinnamon capsules have natural analgesic properties, providing quick relief from abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as gastritis or ulcers.

Cinnamon fiber: The soluble fiber in Ceylon cinnamon capsules can help slow down the digestion of carbohydrates, reducing blood sugar spikes that can contribute to upper abdominal discomfort and pain.

Methanol extract: The methanol extract of Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux or ulcers and helping to reduce symptoms of upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help to alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and improving digestive health.
Barks: Containing essential oils with analgesic properties, barks provide relief from upper abdominal pain by numbing the affected area and reducing discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinnamomum verum bark in late summer or early fall for optimal volatile oil content.
Dry the harvested cinnamomum verum bark in a well-ventilated area for at least 7 days.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground cinnamomum verum bark with 100mg of filler material such as silica or calcium carbonate.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Galip nut capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain a unique combination of compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The capsules work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and small intestine, providing rapid relief from discomfort and cramping. Additionally, galip nut has natural antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, further alleviating pain and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Galip nut capsules contain flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as gastritis, duodenitis, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Antispasmodic properties: The capsules' alkaloids and flavonoids have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which can help reduce muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines that contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Gastroprotective properties: Galip nut capsules contain compounds that inhibit the production of gastric acid and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach from damage, which can help relieve symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Galip nut plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with upper abdominal pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the Galip nut plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried bark of canarium strictum trees in bulk, ensuring minimal contamination.
Dry the harvested bark further by spreading it out under shade for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried canarium strictum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered canarium strictum bark with 3 parts of empty gelatin capsules, ensuring uniform blending.
Fill the blended mixture into gelatin capsules, sealing each one individually within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the herb angelica sinensis, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The capsules can also help to reduce muscle spasms in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to pain and discomfort in the upper abdomen. Additionally, angelica sinensis has been shown to have a gentle warming effect on the body, which can help to ease cramps and spasms, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds, such as apiol and ligustilide, relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Angelica capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the abdominal region that may be contributing to pain.

Digestive soothing property: Angelica's mucilages and saponins have a protective and soothing effect on the digestive tract lining, reducing irritation and discomfort that can exacerbate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica contain a high concentration of essential oils, including angelic acid and apiol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving upper abdominal pain.

Rhyzomes: Angelica rhyzomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic activities, helping to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves in their entirety for a total of 20 grams.
Grind the plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ground angelica archangelica with two parts fillers such as cornstarch or silica gel to create a 1:2 ratio.
Fill size 0 empty capsules with approximately 250-500 milligrams of the herbal mixture for a total of 30-60 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cassia capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, cassia's antibacterial and antiviral compounds help eliminate harmful gut bacteria and viruses that can cause abdominal pain. As a result, cassia capsules provide natural relief from upper abdominal pain, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cassia capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the upper abdomen by blocking prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.

Antispasmodic properties: The capsules' antispasmodic properties, particularly the presence of lignans, help relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain.
Carminative effects: Cassia capsules have carminative properties, which means they can help reduce gas and bloating in the digestive system, relieving pressure and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing upper abdominal pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing essential oils and phenolic compounds, rhyzomes help to relax the digestive tract muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried senna alexandrina leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity.
Dry the plant material in shade at room temperature for three to four days.
Crush the dried herb into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 400mg of powdered senna alexandrina with 100mg of vegetable stearate and 50mg of silica dioxide.
Fill capsules with the prepared mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain berberine, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Berberine has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which often manifests as abdominal pain. Additionally, goldenseal's natural antibacterial properties can help combat H. pylori infections, a common cause of stomach ulcers and pain. By addressing these underlying issues, goldenseal capsules may provide relief from recurring upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that can help alleviate symptoms of stomach upset and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Beta-carotene: Goldenseal is rich in beta-carotene, a precursor to vitamin A that helps protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract and reduces oxidative stress, which may help soothe and heal irritated stomach tissue causing upper abdominal pain.

Coptisine: Coptisine is a bioactive compound found in goldenseal that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in berberine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving upper abdominal pain.
Rhyzomes: Goldenseal's rhyzomes contain a high concentration of alkaloids, including berberine and hydrastine, which help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the upper abdomen.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 milligrams of dried hydrastis canadensis root powder from a trusted source or produce your own by drying and grinding the roots.
Weigh out 250 milligrams of powdered ginger rhizome to enhance digestive benefits and combine it with the hydrastis canadensis root powder.
Mix the combined herbs with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler setup, heating the mixture for 10 minutes to facilitate herbal extraction.
Allow the herbal extract to steep for 30 minutes before straining the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discarding the solids.

Fill gelatin capsules with the cooled herbal extract, each containing 250 milligrams of the hydrastis canadensis and ginger rhizome combination, and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract. The isoflavones present in red clover have been shown to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders. Additionally, red clover has been found to improve digestion and alleviate symptoms of bloating, gas, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover capsules contain a type of phytoestrogen called isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles, providing relief from stomach cramps and spasms associated with upper abdominal pain.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds present in red clover capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.

Phytoestrogens: As mentioned earlier, isoflavones are a type of phytoestrogen found in red clover capsules, which can help regulate hormone levels and alleviate symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS), such as bloating, cramps, and breast tenderness, all of which can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by digestive issues.
Flowers: Contain isoflavones that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the stomach lining.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried trifolium pratense flowers in sufficient quantity for processing.
Clean and sift the dried flowers to remove any debris or contaminants.
Measure out 100 grams of the cleaned and sifted trifolium pratense flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground trifolium pratense with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create an herbal infusion that steeps for 10 minutes before straining.
Transfer the infused liquid to a capsule machine and fill capsules with the prepared mixture at a dosage of 250 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its unique mucilaginous properties. The capsules contain a soothing gel-like substance that coats the digestive tract, providing relief from inflammation and irritation. This natural anti-inflammatory action helps to calm and protect the lining of the stomach and small intestine, reducing discomfort and pain associated with conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and gastritis. Regular consumption of slippery elm capsules can help to reduce frequency and severity of upper abdominal pain episodes.
remedy_properties: Mucoadhesive properties: Slippery elm's mucilages stick to the lining of the digestive tract, providing a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritation, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the abdominal area by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Sotheing and demulcent effects: Slippery elm's mucilages create a protective film on the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation, inflammation, and discomfort, while its soothing properties calm the stomach lining and reduce spasms that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.

Barks: The barks of the slippery elm plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and small intestine, alleviating upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ulmus rubra bark, organic cornstarch, and gelatin in separate containers.
Measure one teaspoon of dried ulmus rubra bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ulmus rubra bark with 250 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.

Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids, and then add 2 teaspoons of organic cornstarch to thicken the mixture, stirring until dissolved.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the thickened decoction mixture, using approximately 500 milligrams per capsule, and store them in an airtight container for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain a concentrated extract of the herb summer savory, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which is often a primary cause of upper abdominal pain. Additionally, summer savory has been shown to relax the smooth muscle in the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Summer savory capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or gastritis, by reducing swelling and irritation in the stomach lining.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils present in summer savory have a natural ability to release gas bubbles in the digestive tract, helping to relieve cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upper abdominal pain caused by conditions such as indigestion or dyspepsia.

Spasmolytic properties: Summer savory has been found to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by spasms or cramping due to conditions such as acid reflux or gallstones, by reducing muscle contractions and improving digestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and relieving digestive discomfort.
Seeds: Contain a unique compound called carvacrol, which has natural analgesic properties that can help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh satureja hortensis leaves in the morning when they are most potent and dry them using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 2 hours.
Combine 250mg of dried satureja hortensis leaves with 500mg of gelatin powder in a clean glass bowl to prepare the capsule mixture.
Add 10ml of purified water to the bowl and mix well until the mixture forms a uniform paste using a stainless steel spoon for 5 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture, ensuring each capsule contains 250mg of satureja hortensis leaves and seal them immediately after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight glass container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage, ensuring they are kept away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its unique bioactive compound, piperine. Piperine has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and ease digestion-related discomfort in the upper abdomen. Additionally, piperine may also enhance the bioavailability of other nutrients and herbs, promoting overall digestive health and alleviating symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as bloating, gas, and cramps. By supplementing with herbal black pepper capsules, individuals can experience relief from these uncomfortable symptoms and enjoy improved digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of upper abdominal pain such as bloating and cramping.

Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Black pepper contains enzymes that stimulate digestive enzymes in the body, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help relieve symptoms of upper abdominal pain caused by poor digestion or malabsorption.

Gastroprotective Compounds: Herbal black pepper capsules contain compounds such as piperine and caryophyllene, which have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of black pepper plant have been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, including upper abdominal pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, a modified stem that grows underground, contains piperine, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that can help alleviate upper abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect and clean high-quality black pepper fruits in a dry place away from direct sunlight for at least seven days.
Grind the dried black pepper fruits into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about five minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered black pepper with two parts of excipient such as silica or calcium carbonate to fill capsules.
Fill empty vegetable-based gelatin capsules with the prepared black pepper mixture and seal them immediately to maintain potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place away from children and pets for up to six months.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upper abdominal pain that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues such as constipation, bloating, and indigestion. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like Triphala, Ginger, and Chamomile may help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, herbal remedies for irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and stomach ulcers may also provide relief from upper abdominal discomfort. These natural remedies often work by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and improving gut health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with upper abdominal pain is a blend of Ginger, Turmeric, and Peppermint. Ginger helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, while Turmeric's active compound, Curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the abdomen and improves digestion, reducing cramps and discomfort. Taking these herbal capsules together can provide quick relief from upper abdominal pain, promoting a healthy digestive system and overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, herbal agents have shown potential in managing visceral hypersensitivity, a common cause of upper abdominal pain. The study, which extracted data from clinical and animal studies published between 2004 and 2020, found that herbal agents such as Radix, Melissia, Glycyrrhizae, Mentha, and Liquorice were effective in managing VH in conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and dyspepsia. By modulating the expression of TRPV1 and suppressing 5-HT3 receptors, these herbal agents can provide relief from upper abdominal pain caused by VH.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea, digestive discomfort, and cramping associated with upper abdominal pain. The capsules help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach, promoting a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system. Additionally, ginger has natural antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the abdominal region, providing fast relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from pain associated with conditions like gastritis and dyspepsia.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate upper abdominal pain and discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.

Zingerone: This compound has a carminative effect, helping to relieve upper abdominal pain and discomfort by reducing gas and bloating in the digestive system, which can contribute to conditions like indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale, rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes that help to reduce inflammation and pain in the upper abdomen.

Stems: While less commonly used than rhyzomes and roots, the stems of Zingiber officinale may be used in some herbal preparations to help reduce upper abdominal pain, although their effectiveness is less well-studied.
remedy_recipe: Grind 250mg of dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 100mg of herbal excipients such as cellulose or tapioca starch in a bowl.
Add 10-20 drops of vegetable glycerin to the powder mixture to aid in capsule formation.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100-200 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin, present in these capsules, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may alleviate symptoms of conditions such as gastritis and ulcers. Additionally, curcumin may also enhance digestion and reduce oxidative stress, leading to a decrease in upper abdominal discomfort and pain. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to soothe and protect the digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps reduce inflammation and relieve upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It helps alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, and also exhibits antioxidant properties.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It helps in reducing upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, and also exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are sometimes used due to their lower cost and higher bioavailability of curcumin compared to rhizomes, providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Dry 2-3 kg of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes at 50-60°c for 8-10 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind 500g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250g of powder with 50g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin in a bowl to create the capsule mixture.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 1000 capsules using a consistent pressure setting.
Store 500 capsules in airtight containers and keep them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Liquiritigenin: It helps with upper abdominal pain by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause upper abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
Leaves: The leaves contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, but also have other bioactive compounds that contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a clean environment to ensure quality control.
Crush the harvested roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 20 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to create a uniform solution.
Fill 100-capsule gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the solution each and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. Eugenol: This compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the upper abdominal region, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Collect 500g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from a reputable supplier to ensure quality and purity.
Grind 150g of the dried bark into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100g of vegetable cellulose and 50g of silicon dioxide in a large bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and fill 1000 capsules in 10 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they improve blood circulation to the digestive system. Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are rich in bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate upper abdominal pain.

Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially providing relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems from a reputable supplier to ensure quality.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part ginkgo biloba powder with 2 parts filler material such as rice flour or potato starch.
Use a capsule machine to fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules at a dosage of 240mg.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain senna glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help to relieve constipation. Seeds: The seeds of Cassia angustifolia are commonly used to make capsules for upper abdominal pain as they contain sennosides, which help in relieving constipation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of dried cassia angustifolia leaves and flowers that have been properly identified and dried.
Grind 200 grams of the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the ground cassia angustifolia powder with 90 grams of cellulose powder or other capsule filler.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the mixed powder and seal them using a capsule filler machine.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place away from light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they are rich in antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powder with a small amount of cornstarch or cellulose powder as a filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain a rich source of essential oils, primarily anethole, which exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and relieve upper abdominal pain due to their carminative properties.

Grind the sifted foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a small bowl for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with upper abdominal pain because they contain the bioactive compound piperine, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Grind 10 grams of the dried piper nigrum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Mix the powdered piper nigrum with 1 teaspoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and compress each capsule according to manufacturer instructions.

Furthermore, Panax ginseng has been traditionally used to improve digestion and boost the immune system, potentially contributing to its benefits in addressing upper abdominal pain. Its potential to regulate gut function may also play a role in alleviating discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system.

Ginsenolic acid: This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve upper abdominal pain by blocking pain signals and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
Saponins: These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the digestive system, reducing upper abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate abdominal pain.
Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are used to make capsules as they contain bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, such as upper abdominal pain.

Stems: The stems of Panax ginseng are used to make capsules, which contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate abdominal pain and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500mg of dried panax ginseng root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ginseng powder with 500mg of vegetable cellulose in a mixing bowl for 2 minutes.
Add 10ml of glycerin to the mixture and mix for 3 minutes using a stainless steel spoon.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and press the capsules for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal panax ginseng capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
